steps.title: Find the two radii
steps.content: The outside diameter is $60$ inches, so the outside radius is $30$ inches. The wall thickness is $\frac{5}{8}$ inch, so the inside radius is $$30-\frac{5}{8}=29.375$$

steps.title: Use annulus volume
steps.content:
The wall volume is the outer cylinder volume minus the inner cylinder volume.
$$V=\pi h(R^2-r^2)$$
$$V=\pi(144)(30^2-29.375^2)$$

steps.title: Evaluate the volume
steps.content:
Compute the difference and multiply by the height.
$$30^2-29.375^2=37.109375$$
$$144(37.109375)\pi$$ is about $16{,}788$.
The answer is D.
